name: Haiti

colors.symbolism: the white panel bearing the coat of arms; symbolises the harmony between the country's communities
elements of the coat of arms (palm tree, mound)
elements of the coat of arms (cannons, cap of liberty)
details and outlines in the coat of arms

description: Two equal horizontal stripes — blue above, red below. The state flag bears the national coat of arms on a small white panel at the centre, depicting a palm tree topped with a Phrygian cap of liberty surrounded by trophies of war and the motto L'UNION FAIT LA FORCE. The civil flag is the plain bicolour.
history: The blue-and-red bicolour was first used by Dessalines on 18 May 1803 at the Congress of Arcahaie. After many changes — including a black-and-red flag under Henri Christophe (1806–1820) and again under François Duvalier (1964–1986) — the original blue-and-red was restored by the Constitution of 1987, which formalised the design currently in use. The flag dates from 25 February 1986, when the original blue and red were re-adopted following the fall of Jean-Claude Duvalier; the constitutional definition followed in 1987.

features.symbolism: the blue-and-red bicolour represents the union of the Black majority (blue) and the people of mixed race (red); created in May 1803 by Jean-Jacques Dessalines who, by tradition, ripped the white from the French Tricolour and had Catherine Flon sew the remaining halves together to symbolise rejection of French rule

features.role: national coat of arms (state flag only)
features.symbolism: a green mound bearing a palm tree topped by a Phrygian cap of liberty, flanked by drapery, drums, trumpets, anchors, cannons and cannonballs; below a white ribbon with the motto L'UNION FAIT LA FORCE ('Union makes strength'). The coat of arms appears on the state flag; the civil flag is the plain blue-and-red bicolour
